segments.text: At that time shall Michael stand up, the great prince which standeth for the children of thy people: and there shall be a time of trouble, such as never was since there was a nation even to that same time: and at that time thy people shall be delivered, every one that shall be found written in the book.
segments.speaker: Angel

segments.text: Many of them that sleep in the dust of the earth shall awake, some to everlasting life, and some to shame and everlasting contempt.
segments.speaker: Angel

segments.text: They that be wise shall shine as the brightness of the firmament; and they that turn many to righteousness as the stars for ever and ever.
segments.speaker: Angel

segments.text: Shut up the words, and seal the book, even to the time of the end: many shall run to and fro, and knowledge shall be increased.
segments.speaker: Divine Being

segments.text: Then I Daniel looked, and, behold, there stood other two, the one on this side of the bank of the river, and the other on that side of the bank of the river.
How long shall it be to the end of these wonders?
segments.speaker: Unknown Angel

segments.text: It shall be for a time, times, and an half; and when he shall have accomplished to scatter the power of the holy people, all these things shall be finished.
segments.speaker: Man Clothed in Linen

segments.text: I heard, but I understood not:
O my Lord, what shall be the end of these things?
segments.speaker: Daniel

segments.text: Go thy way, Daniel: for the words are closed up and sealed till the time of the end.
segments.speaker: Divine Being

segments.text: Many shall be purified, and made white, and tried; but the wicked shall do wickedly: and none of the wicked shall understand; but the wise shall understand.
segments.speaker: Divine Being

segments.text: From the time that the daily sacrifice shall be taken away, and the abomination that maketh desolate set up, there shall be a thousand two hundred and ninety days.
segments.speaker: Divine Being

footnotes: the abomination: Heb. to set up the abomination

segments.text: Blessed is he that waiteth, and cometh to the thousand three hundred and five and thirty days.
segments.speaker: Divine Being

segments.text: Go thou thy way till the end be: for thou shalt rest, and stand in thy lot at the end of the days.
segments.speaker: Divine Being

footnotes: for thou: or, and thou, etc
